methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $70M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $120M - $5.0B.

data.narrative:
Neustark, a Series B climate and energy technology company, is evaluated against a universe of 9 comparable companies—8 private firms and 1 public entity (LNZA). The peer set for same-sector, same-stage analysis comprises Commonwealth Fusion Systems ($5.0B), Pano AI ($500M), Sage Geosystems ($400M), Reverion ($310M), and CarbonCapture Inc. ($300M), among others, with a median valuation of $355M and range of $120M to $5.0B.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, treating the company's $70M in total raised as a valuation proxy, applies a 1.9x multiplier typical for Series B advancement, yielding an implied value of $133M. Peer-median analysis directly references the 6-company same-stage, same-sector cohort, implying $355M. The resulting triangulated range spans $113M (low) to $408M (high), with a median of $244M across both methodologies.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $113M to $408M, centered near $244M. Key uncertainties include the material dispersion within the peer set—driven partly by Commonwealth Fusion's $5.0B outlier valuation—and the appropriateness of stage-progression multipliers absent Neustark-specific growth metrics or capital deployment efficiency data. Resolution of sector-specific deployment risk and competitive positioning within carbon removal infrastructure would refine the range substantially.
